name: Ernie Caceres
abstract: Ernesto "Ernie" Caceres (November 22, 1911 – January 10, 1971) was an American jazz saxophonist born in Rockport, Texas. He was a member of the Glenn Miller Orchestra from 1940 to 1942.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: College Jazz Goes to Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: There was Ernie Caceres ... and Will Bradley ... and we had a hell of a band, 32 pieces

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And that's where I first heard Ernie Caceres, the great baritone saxophone player. In fact, he seemed to be more advanced musically than the other guys that he was playing with. That's how he really impressed me when I heard him play, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer states: 'I know that you’re fond of Ernie Caceres in his day.' Interviewee responds: 'Oh yeah, Ernie Caceres...'
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I think I replaced Ernie Caceres who was in the band. Muggsy had a big band at that time
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Miff Mole and Ernie Caceres I had... We just went up there and did a one night gig.
